meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: User Onboarding for Students: Overcome Basic App Limits
content.seo_description: Break past basic app limitations. Learn how to implement professional user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide for students.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because your apps lack a 'sticky' first-run experience. By implementing a structured user onboarding flow, you guide users to their 'Aha!' moment immediately. Using Antigravity to scaffold the UI and Claude Code to handle the logic, you transform a static interface into a professional, conversion-driven product.
content.intro:
The screen glows, your code compiles, but nobody stays. You’ve built the functionality, yet your app feels like a ghost town because users drop off within seconds of landing. It is the classic 'student trap'—focusing on logic at the expense of the user's journey. You aren't failing as a developer; you are just missing the bridge between code and human experience.

Great software doesn't just work; it welcomes. By integrating a guided onboarding flow, you turn a confusing feature set into a narrative. Today, we bridge that gap. We will leverage high-velocity tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to build an onboarding experience that not only retains users but showcases your engineering maturity to future employers.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single action that delivers value to your user. Onboarding exists to drive that specific action, not to show every feature you built.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user journey map for a task management app that highlights the 'create first task' action as the primary conversion goal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current codebase and suggest the most critical user action for first-time retention.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding State
steps.description: Create a conditional rendering layer that checks if a user has completed the onboarding sequence, preventing them from seeing the empty dashboard prematurely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component structure for an onboarding modal that tracks completion status using local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a persistent onboarding state manager in my App.tsx file.'

steps.title: Interactive Tooltips & Walkthroughs
steps.description: Use lightweight UI components to guide the user's eye toward your primary call-to-action button, reducing friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable tooltip component that highlights a specific DOM element and triggers a callback on click.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Add a step-by-step tour guide that highlights the create-task button using a focus-overlay effect.'

steps.title: Progress Tracking
steps.description: Humans hate open loops. Add a progress bar to your onboarding to gamify the setup process and increase completion rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a linear progress bar component that updates based on the current step index of the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate a progress bar that tracks the 3-step onboarding sequence completion.'

steps.title: Telemetry and Feedback
steps.description: Track where users drop off. If they close the modal at step 2, your instructions are likely too complex.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a simple logging utility to track which step of the onboarding flow a user completes or skips.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement console logging for onboarding step completion metrics to track user drop-off points.'

pro_tips: Never ask for email sign-up before delivering value; let the user experience the core app first.
Use 'Empty States' as onboarding opportunities—don't show blank screens, show an invite to create something.
Keep onboarding to 3 steps maximum; anything longer increases the bounce rate exponentially.
Allow an 'I am an expert' skip button to respect power users who hate tutorials.
Test your onboarding flow by recording a screen capture of a friend using it; watch where their mouse hovers, not what they say.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, deploy your app to Vercel or Netlify, share the link in our community showcase, and ask for a 'usability audit' from a peer.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are a product builder who understands the user journey. Now that you have the tools and the framework, it is time to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
